About: It is about the Battle of Leipzig.
AssociatedWith: It is associated with the Battle of Leipzig in 1813.
ConsistsOf: It consists of two 22" x 34" 6-color mapsheets.
It consists of 400 1/2" counters, including 90 blanks.
It consists of an 8-page rulebook.
It consists of a 32-page study folder.
It consists of an errata card.
It consists of a 6-sided die.
It consists of three maps (two 22" x 34", one 17" x 22").
It consists of 560 1/2" counters.
It consists of a 22-page rulebook.
It consists of a study folder.
DevelopedBy: It was developed by Kevin Zucker.
HasA: It has an "I Go, You Go", alternating series of turns.
It has Army Commanders that use Officers to transmit orders.
It has ratings for commanders.
It has a general order issued every four hours.
It has rules for units in reserve to move free of command restrictions.
It has rules for cavalry to conduct charges.
It has rules for a token attack with experimental Congreve rockets.
It has a choice of Basic rules, Campaign rules and Grand Tactical rules.
It has a larger map for more maneuvers.
It has the clash at Hanau included as a bonus.
HasFeatureOf: It is characterized by an "I Go, You Go" alternating series of turns.
It is characterized by advanced leadership rules.
It is characterized by simple basic structure.
It is characterized by campaign game's Allied victory condition around the destruction of the Leipzig bridge.
It is characterized by darker-colored counters.
It is characterized by physically disappointing game components.
It is characterized by excellent historical notes and a good order of battle.
It is characterized by new command control system.
It is characterized by updated game system.
InfluencedBy: It was influenced by the mechanism in War Between The States.
It was influenced by Napoleon's Last Battles.
KnownFor: It is known for simulating the Battle of Leipzig.
It is known for its advanced leadership rules.
It is known for its "I Go, You Go" alternating series of turns.
It is known for its choice of Basic, Campaign, and Grand Tactical rules.
It is known for its inclusion of the clash at Hanau as a bonus.
It is known for its updated game system.
ProducedBy: It was produced by Operational Studies Group.
It was produced by Clash of Arms.
PublishedAs: It was published as the 5th edition in 2013.
PublishedBy: It was published by Operational Studies Group.
It was published by Clash of Arms.
UsedFor: It is used for simulating the Battle of Leipzig.
It is used for transmitting orders to move and fight.
It is used for illustrating the command system of the Napoleonic era.
UsedTo: It is used to transmit orders to move and fight.
It is used to simulate the Battle of Leipzig.
It is used to illustrate the command system of the Napoleonic era.
WonBy: It was won by the Charles S. Roberts Award for Best Pre-20th Century Game of 1979.
